finding_results.notes: The closing threat now stays on Cliff as a reachable body and on the dock as a private setting, then hands off to the 10:30 dock meeting without spelling out submersion mechanics or an accident-engineering scenario.
The twenty-five-year continuity now belongs to Tommy's rail-side ritual rather than to the specific post-fire Adirondack chair.

finding_results.notes: The cited fragment clusters were rebuilt into complete-sentence narration while keeping Tommy's blunt pressure and concrete noun bias.

Chapter 12 now frames Tommy's fire recollection as partial embodied memory rather than as a clean duplicate of Martin's written RT account, and it explicitly distinguishes the sealed document from Tommy's recall. Full canonical reconciliation of the on-page document itself still requires edits outside this chapter.
Tommy's memory now aligns the truck as blue while allowing the firelight to skew how it looked in the moment.

This chapter no longer assigns Martin a conflicting specific post-fire cleanup role and instead separates Tommy's guilt-memory from Martin's later written account. The direct contradiction between the chapter 8 and chapter 16 document reads remains outside chapter-12-only scope.

The chapter now runs 5543 words. Added material is causal rather than filler: an expanded fire-memory section, the deck aftermath, the dinner pressure sequence, Tommy publicly backing Cliff's debt claim, and Cliff setting the 10:30 dock meeting on Nate's live phone.

summary: Expanded chapter 12 to 5543 words, corrected the chair and truck continuity issues, rebuilt the cited fragment-heavy passages into full-sentence narration, and replaced the overexplicit dock-close with a more dangerous but less named threat. The new dinner/aftermath material turns Cliff's leverage into live social pressure, forces Tommy to back the debt claim in front of Paul and Joel, and seeds the later dock meeting. Tommy's fire memory is now explicitly partial and distinct from Martin's written RT account, but full fire-document reconciliation across other chapters remains pending.
